executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 99 Canadian organizations publicly breached on June 15th alone, spanning critical sectors including healthcare, education, financial services, and public infrastructure. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (coinbasecartel), National Money Mart with database leaked (Everest), Eastern Townships School Board (Qilin), Peterborough Public Health (Lynx), and Municipality of North Perth (WorldLeaks). The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 18 victims, followed by Clop (13), Play (11), and SafePay (10). Multiple law firms, CPAs, municipalities, and healthcare providers have been compromised, representing a coordinated mass exploitation event requiring immediate incident response across all sectors.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a mass ransomware event with 99 organizations breached on June 15, 2026. Critical infrastructure impacted includes Peterborough Public Health (Lynx), Municipality of North Perth (WorldLeaks), MRC de Maskinongé district (Medusa), Eastern Townships School Board (Qilin), and Eeyou Communications Network telecom provider (AiLock). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose breached by CoinbaseCartel ransomware gang; Active Green + Ross automotive retail chain breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company database leaked by Everest ransomware group; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi; Trucash financial services breached by Lynx; multiple accounting/CPA firms compromised including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), Reidhurst Nagy (IncRansom)

report_2w.executive_summary: Three critical zero-day vulnerabilities are actively exploited in the wild: CVE-2026-10520 (Ivanti Sentry, max severity), CVE-2026-35273 (Oracle PeopleSoft exploited by ShinyHunters threat actor), and CVE-2026-50751 (Check Point VPN authentication bypass with public PoC). ShinyHunters breached over 100 organizations including major U.S. universities and is now listing American Tower Corporation, JCPenney, and other high-profile victims. French government secure messaging was breached affecting 73,000 employees, and pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk disclosed a breach of clinical trial patient data. A new self-propagating ransomware called The Gentlemen has claimed 478 victims and spreads like a worm.

canada_landscape.summary: Limited Canadian-specific activity observed during this period. One Mastodon post referenced Canadian cybersecurity context in relation to the Mythos security story, but no direct CCCS advisories or major Canadian organizational breaches were reported in the collected sources. No incidents affecting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified.
